data.name: Text_NoMonitorsDiscovered
data.comment: Text-mode output when list/get finds no monitors.

data.name: Text_NotSupported
data.comment: Text-mode marker for an unsupported setting.

data.name: Text_Unknown
data.comment: Text-mode marker for a supported setting whose value was not read.

data.name: Text_Failed
data.comment: Text-mode marker that an apply-profile setting failed at the hardware.

data.name: Text_NotConnectedSkipped
data.comment: apply-profile: a monitor named by the profile is not currently connected.

data.name: Text_NoSettingsInProfile
data.comment: apply-profile: the profile entry for a connected monitor had no values.

data.name: Text_OutOfRangeSkipped
data.comment: apply-profile: a profile value was outside the valid range and was not written.

data.name: Text_NoProfilesSaved
data.comment: profiles command: no saved profiles exist.

data.name: Text_NoValuesReported
data.comment: capabilities: a discrete VCP code advertised no enumerated values.

data.name: Text_AppliedProfile
data.comment: {0} = profile name.

data.name: Warn_MonitorNumberIgnored
data.value: warning: --monitor-number {0} ignored because --monitor-id was also provided
data.comment: {0} = monitor number. Flag names are CLI syntax and must not be translated.

data.name: Error_NoSettingSpecified
data.value: no setting specified; pass one of --brightness/--contrast/--volume/--color-temperature/--input-source/--power-state/--orientation
data.comment: The flag names are CLI syntax and must not be translated.

data.name: Error_OnlyOneSetting
data.value: only one setting may be applied per 'set' call
data.comment: 'set' is a literal command name and must not be translated.

data.name: Hint_OnlyOneSetting
data.value: split into multiple invocations: one --<setting> per call

data.name: Error_UnknownSetting
data.comment: {0} = the setting name the user passed to --setting.

data.name: Hint_ValidSettings
data.comment: {0} = comma-separated canonical setting names (CLI syntax, not translated).

data.name: Error_TimedOut
data.comment: {0} = number of seconds.

data.name: Error_UnexpectedError
data.comment: {0} = exception message.

data.name: Error_ProviderUnavailable
data.value: PowerDisplay is not running. Enable it in PowerToys settings.
data.comment: Shown when the CLI cannot reach the PowerDisplay app over the IPC pipe.

data.name: Error_DeserializeMismatch
data.value: Response could not be deserialized as expected type.
data.comment: Shown when the app's IPC response does not match the CLI's expected schema (version skew).

data.name: Error_NegativeTimeout
data.value: --timeout must be >= 0 (use 0 to disable the timeout).
data.comment: --timeout is CLI syntax and must not be translated.
